children.label: A
children.text: data on race, ethnicity, sex, primary language, and disability status for applicants, recipients, or participants;

children.label: B
children.text: data at the smallest geographic level such as State, local, or institutional levels if such data can be aggregated;

children.label: C
children.text: sufficient data to generate statistically reliable estimates by racial, ethnic, sex, primary language, and disability status subgroups for applicants, recipients or participants using, if needed, statistical oversamples of these subpopulations; and

children.label: D
children.text: any other demographic data as deemed appropriate by the Secretary regarding health disparities.

children.label: A
children.text: use Office of Management and Budget standards, at a minimum, for race and ethnicity measures;

children.label: B
children.text: develop standards for the measurement of sex, primary language, and disability status;

children.label: i
children.text: collects self-reported data by the applicant, recipient, or participant; and

children.label: ii
children.text: collects data from a parent or legal guardian if the applicant, recipient, or participant is a minor or legally incapacitated;

children.label: i
children.text: locations where individuals with disabilities access primary, acute (including intensive), and long-term care;

children.label: ii
children.text: the number of providers with accessible facilities and equipment to meet the needs of the individuals with disabilities, including medical diagnostic equipment that meets the minimum technical criteria set forth in ; andsection 794f of title 29

children.label: iii
children.text: the number of employees of health care providers trained in disability awareness and patient care of individuals with disabilities; and

children.label: E
children.text: require that any reporting requirement imposed for purposes of measuring quality under any ongoing or federally conducted or supported health care or public health program, activity, or survey includes requirements for the collection of data on individuals receiving health care items or services under such programs activities by race, ethnicity, sex, primary language, and disability status.1So in original.1

children.label: A
children.text: develop national standards for the management of data collected; and

children.label: B
children.text: develop interoperability and security systems for data management.

children.heading: In general2So in original. No par. (2) has been enacted.2
children.label: 1 In general2So in original. No par. (2) has been enacted.2
children.text: For each federally conducted or supported health care or public health program or activity, the Secretary shall analyze data collected under paragraph (a) to detect and monitor trends in health disparities (as defined for purposes of section 285t of this title) at the Federal and State levels.3See References in Text note below.3

children.label: A
children.text: the Office of Minority Health;

children.label: B
children.text: the National Center on Minority Health and Health Disparities;

children.label: C
children.text: the Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality;

children.label: D
children.text: the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention;

children.label: E
children.text: the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services;

children.label: F
children.text: the Indian Health Service and epidemiology centers funded under the Indian Health Care Improvement Act [ et seq.];25 U.S.C. 1601

children.label: G
children.text: the Office of Rural health;5So in original. Probably should be “Health;”.5

children.label: H
children.text: other agencies within the Department of Health and Human Services; and

children.label: I
children.text: other entities as determined appropriate by the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: public postings on the Internet websites of the Department of Health and Human Services; and

children.label: B
children.text: any other reporting or dissemination mechanisms determined appropriate by the Secretary.

children.heading: Availability of data
children.label: 3 Availability of data
children.text: The Secretary may make data described in (a) and (b) available for additional research, analyses, and dissemination to other Federal agencies, non-governmental entities, and the public, in accordance with any Federal agency’s data user agreements.

children.heading: Limitations on use of data
children.label: d Limitations on use of data
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to permit the use of information collected under this section in a manner that would adversely affect any individual.

children.heading: Protection and sharing of data
children.label: e Protection and sharing of data
i
children.text: under privacy protections that are at least as broad as those that the Secretary applies to other health data under the regulations promulgated under section 264(c) of the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act of 1996 (; ); andPublic Law 104–191110 Stat. 2033

children.label: ii
children.text: from all inappropriate internal use by any entity that collects, stores, or receives the data, including use of such data in determinations of eligibility (or continued eligibility) in health plans, and from other inappropriate uses, as defined by the Secretary; and

children.label: B
children.text: all appropriate information security safeguards are used in the collection, analysis, and sharing of data collected pursuant to subsection (a).

children.heading: Data sharing
children.label: 2 Data sharing
children.text: The Secretary shall establish procedures for sharing data collected pursuant to subsection (a), measures relating to such data, and analyses of such data, with other relevant Federal and State agencies including the agencies, centers, and entities within the Department of Health and Human Services specified in subsection (c)(1)..1

children.heading: Data on rural underserved populations
children.label: f Data on rural underserved populations
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure that any data collected in accordance with this section regarding racial and ethnic minority groups are also collected regarding underserved rural and frontier populations.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: g Authorization of appropriations
children.text: For the purpose of carrying out this section, there are authorized to be appropriated such sums as may be necessary for each of fiscal years 2010 through 2014.

children.heading: Requirement for implementation
children.label: h Requirement for implementation
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this section, data may not be collected under this section unless funds are directly appropriated for such purpose in an appropriations Act.

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: i Consultation
children.text: The Secretary shall consult with the Director of the Office of Personnel Management, the Secretary of Defense, the Secretary of Veterans Affairs, the Director of the Bureau of the Census, the Commissioner of Social Security, and the head of other appropriate Federal agencies in carrying out this section.
